summary:
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Advisories - 2026, directness=indirect, why=URL structure implies 2026 advisories exist but content is not rendered; likely the target page for May 2026 Strait of Hormuz threat level, per query intent; next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/advisories/2026  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=UKMTO Products, directness=indirect, why=Hosts Advisories section; confirms advisories are published under /ukmto-products/advisories/, supporting that /advisories/2026 is the correct path; next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Contact Us, directness=high, why=Provides verified emergency and general contact channels to verify advisory status directly; critical for confirming unlisted or unpublished threat levels; next=https://www.ukmto.org/contact-us  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=About UKMTO, directness=medium, why=Establishes UKMTO as authoritative source for maritime security intel in Voluntary Reporting Area (VRA), including Strait of Hormuz; validates source credibility for threat assessments; next=https://www.ukmto.org/about-us  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Warnings - 2024, directness=indirect, why=Demonstrates archive structure for prior year advisories/warnings; implies 2026 advisories will follow same pattern; next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/warnings/2024  

No direct advisory for May 2026 Strait of Hormuz threat level found. The 2026 advisories page (https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/advisories/2026) is the primary target but returns no content — likely unpublished or not yet live. Contact UKMTO via emergency line (+44 (0) 2392 222060) or watchkeepers@ukmto.org to verify if a critical threat level has been issued or is pending.
